'''Ratu Siva Naulago''' (born 6 August 1991) is a Fijian professional [[rugby union]] footballer who plays as a [[Rugby union positions#Wing|winger]] for the [[Bristol Bears]] in the [[Gallagher Premiership Rugby]].

He previously played [[rugby league]] for [[Hull F.C.]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Naulago was born in [[Fiji]].

Naulago is a Private in the [[Yorkshire Regiment]] and served in Cyprus for two years before being based in Warminster, Wiltshire, and playing rugby union as a 'guest player' for [[Bath Rugby]] and [[Saracens F.C.]] Sevens team, who he helped to the Premiership title.

==Playing career==
===Rugby league===
In 2019 he made his [[Super League]] debut for [[Hull F.C.]] against the [[Wigan Warriors]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/47294114|title=Super League: Wigan Warriors V Hull FC|work=BBC Sport |date=22 February 2019 |publisher=BBC}}</ref>

===Rugby union===
In May 2020 it was announced that Naulago would switch codes and join rugby union side [[Bristol Bears]] ahead of the 2020–21 season.<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bristolpost.co.uk/sport/rugby/bristol-bears-ratu-naulago-premiership-4145121|work=Bristol Post|title=Bristol Bears add another Fijian star to their ranks as Ratu Naulago signs from Hull FC|date=10 May 2020}}</ref>
